testCases.query: Build a RAG pipeline that answers questions with citations
expectedBehavior: Retrieves relevant documents with vector similarity search against the embedding index
Constructs numbered context references like [1], [2] for inline citation tracking
Generates with system prompt constraining to context
Returns inline citations and source list

testCases.query: Combine vector and keyword search for better coverage
expectedBehavior: Implements Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to merge results from multiple retrieval methods
Uses k=60 smoothing constant in the RRF formula for balanced rank weighting
Retrieves from both semantic and keyword methods
Merges results by document ID to deduplicate across vector and keyword retrievers

testCases.query: How do I prevent hallucinations when context is insufficient?
expectedBehavior: Implements a context sufficiency check before generation to prevent hallucinated answers
Abstains when confidence > 0.7 and context insufficient
Keeps context under 75% of model limit
Prioritizes highest-relevance documents first when fitting context within the token budget

testCases.query: Design a composable RAG pipeline with multiple stages
expectedBehavior: Follows Decompose → HyDE → Retrieve → Rerank → Fit → Generate order
Each stage is optional and composable
Uses RRF for multi-query fusion to combine results from decomposed sub-queries
Implements timeout fallbacks for slow stages

testCases.query: Which embedding model should I use for production?
expectedBehavior: Recommends text-embedding-3-small for general purpose use with good cost-performance balance
Lists embedding model dimensions and per-token costs for informed model selection
Shows batch embedding API usage to reduce latency and cost for bulk operations
Mentions Ollama as the recommended option for local development and CI embedding generation

testCases.query: How should I chunk documents for embedding?
expectedBehavior: Recommends chunk sizes of 256-1024 tokens with 512 as the typical default setting
Uses 10-20% token overlap between chunks for cross-boundary context continuity
Prefers splitting at semantic boundaries like paragraphs over arbitrary fixed-size character splits
Includes source metadata with chunks such as document ID, page number, and section title

testCases.query: How do I cache embeddings and reduce costs?
expectedBehavior: Implements a Redis-based embedding cache to avoid recomputing embeddings for known content
Uses batch processing with rate limiting
Mentions Matryoshka dimension reduction for lowering storage costs while preserving retrieval quality
Avoids re-embedding unchanged content by checking content hashes before calling the embedding API

testCases.query: Chunks lose context when retrieved — how to fix?
expectedBehavior: Prepends situational context to chunks so they remain meaningful when retrieved in isolation
Uses Claude for generating concise situational context sentences per chunk during indexing
Uses prompt caching for 90% cost reduction
Context is 1-2 sentences focused on retrieval

testCases.query: What's the best retrieval technique according to Anthropic?
expectedBehavior: Combines contextual embeddings with BM25 keyword search in a hybrid retrieval approach
40% BM25 / 60% vector weight split
Achieves a 67% reduction in retrieval failures compared to standard embedding-only search
Adding reranking reduces failures further to 1.3%

testCases.query: Build a complete contextual retrieval system
expectedBehavior: Indexes documents with contextual chunks that include surrounding document-level context
Builds hybrid BM25 + vector retriever
Processes chunks in parallel with semaphore
Uses prompt caching for cost reduction

testCases.query: Queries don't match document terminology — how to improve retrieval?
expectedBehavior: Generates a hypothetical answer document that mirrors the expected retrieval target language
Embeds the hypothetical doc, NOT the query
Uses a fast model like gpt-5.2-mini to generate hypothetical documents with low latency
Sets temperature to 0.3 for consistent and focused hypothetical document generation output

testCases.query: Apply HyDE to each concept in a multi-topic query
expectedBehavior: Decomposes the multi-topic query into individual concepts before applying HyDE to each
Generates HyDE per concept in parallel
Caches HyDE generation results with a content hash key to avoid redundant LLM calls
Uses asyncio.gather for parallelism to run HyDE generation across concepts concurrently

testCases.query: What if HyDE generation is too slow?
expectedBehavior: Implements a 2-3 second timeout for HyDE generation to bound worst-case latency
Falls back to direct query embedding
Uses asyncio.timeout for clean timeout handling
Logs fallback events for monitoring to track how often HyDE timeouts occur in production

testCases.query: Filter out irrelevant documents before generation
expectedBehavior: Grades documents with binary yes/no score
Filters the retrieved set to only include documents graded as relevant by the evaluator
Triggers web search if >50% filtered out
Tracks per-document relevance scores for debugging and retrieval quality analysis

testCases.query: Build a self-correcting RAG with web fallback
expectedBehavior: Implements the Corrective RAG (CRAG) workflow with LangGraph for self-healing retrieval
Rewrites query up to 2 times before web fallback
Uses Tavily for web search as the external fallback when local retrieval is insufficient
Includes retry_count to prevent infinite loops

testCases.query: When should I use knowledge graph RAG?
expectedBehavior: Recommends knowledge graph RAG for entity-rich domains with complex inter-entity relationships
Combines KG entity lookup with vector search
Uses structured output for entity extraction
Compares Self-RAG, CRAG, GraphRAG, Agentic patterns

testCases.query: Route queries to the best retrieval strategy
expectedBehavior: Routes between direct, HyDE, decompose, web strategies
Uses structured output for query classification
Direct search for simple queries (fastest)
HyDE for vocabulary mismatch, decompose for multi-concept

testCases.query: How do I embed images for cross-modal search?
expectedBehavior: Uses CLIP for image and text embedding
Recommends Voyage multimodal-3 for long docs
Normalizes embedding vectors for cosine similarity to ensure consistent distance measurements
Shows both CLIP and Voyage API usage

testCases.query: How to chunk PDFs with images and tables?
expectedBehavior: Uses PyMuPDF for extracting text and image blocks from PDF document pages
Separates text and image blocks during extraction for independent chunking and embedding
Generates descriptive captions for images using a vision model to enable text-based retrieval
Preserves page numbers for citation so retrieved chunks link back to their source location

testCases.query: Build multimodal RAG with image and text retrieval
expectedBehavior: Searches both text and image embeddings
Deduplicates search results by document ID to avoid showing duplicate content from different modalities
Places images before text in generation prompt
Uses Claude for multimodal generation to synthesize answers from both text and image context

testCases.query: How to detect if a query needs decomposition?
expectedBehavior: Uses heuristic indicators (vs, compared to, affect)
Sub-millisecond fast path for simple queries
Skips LLM decomposition for single-concept queries
Lists query types that do/don't need decomposition

testCases.query: Break complex queries into concepts for better retrieval
expectedBehavior: Uses LLM to extract 2-4 concepts
Retrieves results per decomposed concept in parallel to minimize total search latency
Fuses per-concept retrieval results with Reciprocal Rank Fusion for unified ranking
Caches query decomposition results to avoid redundant LLM calls on repeated queries

testCases.query: Combine query decomposition with HyDE
expectedBehavior: Decomposes first, then HyDE per concept
Runs parallel search with HyDE-generated embeddings across all decomposed concept queries
Fuses multi-source results with Reciprocal Rank Fusion for combined relevance ranking
Notes this is the most expensive path

testCases.query: Improve search precision with reranking
expectedBehavior: Uses cross-encoder model for pair-wise scoring
Recommends ms-marco-MiniLM-L-6-v2 as the default cross-encoder model for fast reranking
Retrieves 50-100 candidate documents then reranks to keep the top 10 most relevant
Lists model options with latency and cost

testCases.query: Use LLM to score document relevance
expectedBehavior: Batches all docs in one LLM call
Parses scores defensively (default 0.5 on error)
Truncates document snippets to 200-400 chars to fit within the reranker context window
Shows the Cohere rerank API as an alternative hosted reranking service option

testCases.query: Combine multiple scoring signals for ranking
expectedBehavior: Weighted average: base + LLM + recency
Uses default weights of 30/50/20 for base relevance, LLM score, and recency signals
Falls back to base ranking on timeout to ensure search results are always returned
Stores individual score components for debugging and tuning the ranking weight distribution

testCases.query: Design a database schema for hybrid search
expectedBehavior: Uses vector(1024) column type with an HNSW index for efficient approximate nearest neighbor search
Pre-computes tsvector as a GENERATED column to avoid runtime text processing during keyword search
Creates GIN index for keyword search
Includes document_id foreign key and content_type column for source tracking and filtered queries

testCases.query: Implement hybrid search with pgvector in SQLAlchemy
expectedBehavior: Uses FULL OUTER JOIN for RRF fusion
Uses a 3x fetch multiplier for coverage to ensure enough candidates survive RRF fusion
Uses plainto_tsquery for user input to safely parse natural language into keyword search queries
Coalesces NULL ranks to 0 in the FULL OUTER JOIN to handle single-source matches correctly

testCases.query: HNSW vs IVFFlat — which pgvector index should I use?
expectedBehavior: Recommends HNSW indexing for production deployments due to superior query performance
Notes 17x faster queries than IVFFlat
Lists m=16 and ef_construction=64 as the recommended default HNSW index build parameters
Mentions iterative_scan for filtered queries to improve recall on highly selective metadata filters

testCases.query: Filter search results by content type and boost by metadata
expectedBehavior: Shows filtered search by content_type to scope results to specific document categories
Sets the similarity threshold at 0.75 to filter out low-relevance results from search output
Metadata boosting adds approximately +6% MRR improvement over pure vector similarity ranking
Compares pgvector vs Redis 8 FT.HYBRID
